headline: Senate punches its ticket for judicial battles ahead with cloture set for federal judge nominations!

senate.lastSessionSummary: The Senate confirmed five International Trade Commission members and 160 military nominations across all service branches. A failed procedural vote blocked consideration of a Medicare prior authorization rule disapproval resolution, and judicial nominations advanced with cloture scheduled for Benjamin Flowers' circuit court bid.

onTheFloor.title: Nomination of Benjamin M. Flowers of Ohio to be United States Circuit Judge for the Sixth Circuit
onTheFloor.status: Cloture vote scheduled for Monday, July 20 at 5:30 PM

onTheFloor.title: Nomination of Kara Marie Westercamp of Virginia to be Judge of the United States Court of International Trade
onTheFloor.status: Cloture motion agreed to 49-48; confirmation vote pending

onTheFloor.title: Congressional disapproval of Medicare prior authorization rule under the WISeR Model

house.lastSessionSummary: The House passed H.R. 5362 naming a VA clinic in Marietta, Georgia after Colonel Michael H. Boyce by a 418-0 vote under suspension of the rules. The chamber continued debate on H.R. 9237, the Take Care of America's Veterans Act, and rejected a motion to recommit the bill 210-211.

onTheFloor.title: Take Care of America's Veterans Act to improve benefits for veterans and Department of Veterans Affairs administration
onTheFloor.status: Further proceedings postponed; motion to recommit rejected 210-211

onTheFloor.title: Naming the Department of Veterans Affairs multispecialty clinic in Marietta, Georgia as the Colonel Michael H. Boyce Department of Veterans Affairs Multispecialty Clinic
onTheFloor.status: Passed 418-0 under suspension of the rules
